open_hitl_questions.topic: OpenReport binding return shape — backward-compat mechanism
open_hitl_questions.status: CLOSED in fix-3 per HITL 2026-05-24. fix-1 had over-strictly closed this against the wrapper option (A), citing 'no new verbs' which is a paraphrase, not the literal § 54 contract ('no API breaks. Bugfix-only.'). Re-read of § 54 admits new methods as patch-safe additive surface. Four shapes re-evaluated: A wrapper return (type-checker friction, isinstance/pickling edge cases), B tuple return (rejected — breaks legacy), C new top-level verb `Engine.open_with_report` / `Engine.openWithReport` (clean but joins five-verb surface), D engine-attached accessor `engine.open_report()` / `engine.openReport()` (CHOSEN). Shape D rationale: zero risk to `Engine.open` (signature unchanged), clean types, slots into the existing engine-attached instrumentation taxonomy alongside `engine.counters()`, `engine.drain()`. Spec edits relocate the report from 'Runtime surface' to 'Engine-attached instrumentation / control' in both `dev/interfaces/python.md` and `dev/interfaces/typescript.md`. Idempotency contract: repeated calls return identical data captured at open time. Full rationale in `dev/plans/0.6.1-implementation.md` § 'Q-002 resolution (HITL 2026-05-24)'.
open_hitl_questions.accessor_ts: engine.openReport(): OpenReport (sync — data captured at open)

open_hitl_questions.topic: Fix B-001 (agent-verify removal-detect base-ref) in 0.6.1 scope?
open_hitl_questions.options: (a) yes — orchestrator spawns one-off WF-fix-style slice before AXIS-E-PIN | (b) no — log under 0.6.x patch chain follow-up
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: (a) — AXIS-E-PIN wires a new test into agent-verify; verify-script should be GREEN before then.

Cited dev/design/engine.md § Engine.open success result + lib.rs:541-548 explicitly.

findings.title: Legacy Engine.open compatibility only partially pinned
findings.addressed_by: fix-1 promoted legacy-callsite regression test to PRIMARY GATE and closed Q-002 against new verbs. fix-3 (HITL 2026-05-24) reopened Q-002 against the strict § 54 text ('no API breaks. Bugfix-only.'), evaluated four shapes, chose Shape D (engine-attached accessor `engine.open_report()` / `engine.openReport()`). `Engine.open` signature stays unchanged from 0.6.0; legacy compat is now structural, not test-only. OPENREPORT-py / OPENREPORT-ts skeletons + plan-doc slice-sequence rows + AC-068c/d wording updated for Shape D.

findings.title: Q-002 reopened and re-resolved as Shape D (HITL 2026-05-24)
findings.addressed_by: Plan-doc § 'Q-002 resolution (HITL 2026-05-24)' added; OPENREPORT-py and OPENREPORT-ts skeletons rewritten to spec the engine-attached accessor; AC-068c/d wording rewritten; slice-sequence rows updated. No codex re-review for this fix — the change is HITL-directed and reduces surface (drops the wrapper-class machinery), not increases it. Codex review for the actual OPENREPORT slices happens per-slice when spawned.
